subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The report is exceptionally well organized: detailed table of contents, numbered sections, consistent headings and clear introductionâbodyâconclusion structure.

Logical progression of investigative steps (introduction â data presentation â box-plot stages â bivariate analysis â regression â conclusion) allows the reader to follow without back-tracking.
Concise linking sentences between major sections maintain coherence and support reader engagement.
weaknesses: Occasional lengthy digressions (textbook definitions, extensive quotations) interrupt the flow.
Repetition in conclusion sections (âOverall conclusionâ vs. âFinal conclusionâ) weakens structural crispness.
Some background material and numeric replicas in graphs are more detailed than necessary, reducing conciseness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the exploration contains a few superfluous passages and minor structural repetitions, overall it demonstrates an exceptional level of organization and coherence that meets the IBâs band-4 descriptors for Presentation. The introduction, body and conclusion are clearly delineated and linked logically throughout.

strengths: Mathematical language and notation are used consistently and accurately throughout (quartiles, IQR, Ï, CV, Râ, regression form).
Multiple representations (tables, boxplots, scatter plots with colour-coding, regression line) effectively support the discussion.
Logical structure of mathematical exposition is very clear: calculations are shown step-by-step with appropriate references to appendices.
weaknesses: Minor typographical slips (e.g. âIORâ instead of âIQRâ) and inconsistent decimal separators appear.
A few graphs and tables lack fully self-contained axis labels or units in captions.
Some formulae are presented in dense inline form and could be broken into numbered steps for readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation and multiple representations, providing very clear communication throughout. The minor notation slips and occasional labelling issues do not obscure understanding, justifying a band-4 score for Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Clear personal narrative about motor-home travel provides a coherent context and motivational framework for each stage of the exploration.
Decisions on sampling frame, outlier removal and error analysis show significant independent thought beyond a purely textbook approach.
Use of internet archives to recover 2019 price data and comparison of coefficients of variation indicate genuine initiative.
weaknesses: Methodological approach remains largely conventional for an SL statistics IA, lacking highly innovative or original techniques.
Personal engagement is evident but does not reach the âoutstandingâ level of originality or depth.
The exploration would benefit from more bespoke methodological choices or novel local data sources to elevate creativity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, consistent with a band-2 performance in Personal Engagement. While the narrative is strong, it stops short of exceptional originality and innovation.

strengths: Results are meaningfully linked to contextual factors (subsidies, taxes, MEDC/LEDC status) and outlier effects are analysed.
The student provides thoughtful commentary on negative correlation, trade-offs between fuel cost and safety, and sample-size limitations.
Outcomes (choice of Latin America, suggestions for future data extrapolation) are clearly evaluated with practical implications.
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely descriptive and lacks probing, critical questions about methodology or alternative models.
The limitations section is left incomplete mid-sentence and needs fuller discussion.
Opportunities for deeper critical analysisâsuch as testing other regression models or reflecting on biasâare not fully exploited.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection demonstrates thoughtful analysis and clear evaluation of outcomes, aligning with a band-2 standard. It falls short of the deep, critical insight and comprehensive future-looking evaluation required for band-3.

strengths: A comprehensive suite of relevant statistical techniques (five-figure summaries, Ï, CV, outlier testing, Pearson r, linear regression) is used to address the research question.
Mathematical procedures are correctly applied with a clear understanding of underlying concepts and appropriate course-level complexity.
Calculations and interpretations are accurate, and results are directly linked back to the aim of examining the fuel-cost/safety relationship.
weaknesses: Minor arithmetic slip-ups (inconsistent rounding) and occasional unit-conversion ambiguities (switching between dollars and cents) occur.
The use of Ï does not clarify whether a population or sample formula is assumed.
No advanced inferential tests (e.g. significance testing of correlation) were performed to enhance the mathematical analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies relevant mathematics effectively and in breadth, showing thorough understanding with only minor errors. This performance aligns with a band-5 level for Use of Mathematics (SL).

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations follow the figures, calculations are shown step-by-step, and referencing to appendices is clear, giving good logical structure.  Verbosity and the presence of copy-pasted formulae with limited adaptation prevent the communication from reaching the âexcellentâ standard.

Calculations of IQR, Ï, CV and Pearson r are mostly correct; minor arithmetic slips (e.g. inconsistent rounding, typographical confusion between 135 and 135.0) do not materially affect conclusions, demonstrating good understanding that is âmostly correctâ.  This aligns with band 4.

Tables, boxplots, scatter plots and a regression line are employed to support discussion; colour-coding by region is appropriate.  Axes and units appear, though some screenshots are blurred and one boxplot label is inaccurate, so the representations are effective but not âhighlyâ so.

The student follows a clear investigative sequence: introduction â data presentation â box-plot stages â bivariate work â regression â conclusion.  Explanations usually connect one step to the next, so the reader can follow the logic without back-tracking.  However, lengthy digressions (e.g. textbook definitions of skewness and extensive quotations) occasionally interrupt the flow, showing good but not âexcellentâ logical development.

Using internet archives to recover 2019 price data, computing percentage error to test extrapolation, and comparing CVs for reliability show creativity and initiative, but these steps are not exceptionally original or sophisticated, just solid extensions of syllabus ideas.

Results are linked to contextual knowledge (MEDC/LEDC, subsidies, taxes) and the effect of removing outliers is analysed.  While thoughtful, the critique is moderate and does not, for instance, test other models or challenge initial assumptions, so the analysis is âthoughtfulâ rather than âdeep and insightfulâ.

The student evaluates outcomes (Latin America as the compromise choice), notes limitations (ignored variables, archive reliability), and suggests future data extrapolation.  This is a clear evaluation with implications but does not reach the comprehensive, forward-looking detail required for band 3.

The student comments on the implication of negative correlation, acknowledges trade-offs between fuel cost and safety, and recognises sample-size issues, providing meaningful reflection.  The discussion remains largely descriptive and lacks probing questions or reconsideration of methodology, so depth is adequate but not âsubstantialâ.

Notation for quartiles, IQR, Ï, CV, R_p, regression form etc. is generally correct and used throughout.  Minor slips (IOR instead of IQR, inconsistent decimal separators) appear but do not impede understanding; hence âmostly consistentâ use.

A personal narrative about motor-home travel frames and motivates every stage, and region choice is justified through that narrative.  This establishes a clear personal approach, yet the bulk of the work still resembles a conventional statistics project, so the strand fits band 2.

Most content is connected to the aim, yet the report is almost 5 000 words and devotes large sections to generic theory, background quotations and very detailed numeric replicas already visible in graphs.  These superfluous passages reduce conciseness; still, outright irrelevance is limited, so the strand fits the âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ description.

Every mathematical element used (boxplots, correlation, regression, CV) is directly relevant to answering the stated research question; no superfluous techniques are introduced.  The range of tools is adequate and applied in context, matching the âused appropriatelyâ descriptor.

The student chooses the problem, designs the sampling frame, and decides to drop outliers, all of which show independent thought beyond following a textbook recipe.  Decisions are, however, standard for an SL statistics IA and not deeply innovative, so evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

A detailed table of contents, numbered sections, and consistent headings demonstrate solid organisation; appendices are referenced from the body text.  Nevertheless some items (e.g. two separate âOverall conclusion/Final conclusionâ) repeat similar material and weaken structural crispness, so the work is âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionallyâ so.
Statistics content is entirely on-syllabus for AA SL and is neither below nor markedly above the expected level; hence âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3).

annotations.comment: In the discussion of positive skewness you refer to âIORâ instead of âIQR.â Correct this typographical slip to maintain notation consistency.
Graph 5 lacks axis labels and units in caption. Include these to improve readability and precise interpretation.
Scatter plot caption on page 11 notes colours but omits axis titles. Ensure all diagrams are fully self-contained.
The formula for R_p is presented correctly; follow with a brief interpretation of each term to aid mathematical communication.
Inconsistent notation appears: âR_nâ is used instead of âR_p.â Standardize symbol usage to avoid confusion.
The coefficient table (Table 6) mislabels the last range as â0.8 to 0.10.â Correct this to â0.8 to 1.0â to align with standard conventions.
The research question is clearly stated but overly verbose. Consider refining it to succinctly specify the dependent and independent variables for improved focus and coherence.
The decision to omit outliers like Venezuela and Ukraine is justified contextually, but explain quantitatively how extreme values would skew your results.
Table 1 clearly presents raw data, but indicate units explicitly in the header (e.g., â$ per literâ) for full clarity in multiple representations.
The aim statement describes intent but lacks conciseness. Reword to directly link low fuel cost and casualty rates in one sentence, then detail the statistical methods.
The personal narrative adds depth to your rationale and demonstrates clear engagement with the context of exploration, linking personal experience to statistical choices.
When describing your sampling method you refer to a ânon-random quota sampling.â Discuss the potential bias this introduces and its impact on validity.
The description of standard deviation and CV is accurate but could benefit from an explicit definition of CV before using the symbol.
The bibliographic citation beneath Table 2 repeats sources; consider integrating source list into a single bibliography and referencing via in-text citations.
The explanation of boxplots introduces key terms well, though a concise definition of skewness (with formula) would strengthen mathematical communication.
Graph 1 illustrates a generic distribution; this conceptual example is tangential to your data. Focus on real sample plots to maintain relevance.
Five-figure summary table lacks a title and caption. Number and label it to match in-text references for clear organization.
Outlier testing uses correct formula, but show intermediate numeric steps explicitly to improve clarity and reproducibility.
Statistical commentary on South America cites Venezuela as outlier; provide exact casualty value and source citation for rigor.
The five-figure summaries for fuel cost switch units to cents mid-analysis; explicitly justify this transformation and reflect on its impact.
Outlier testing roundings are inconsistent (â3.5fâ). Ensure uniform significant figure notation and clarify rounding rationale.
Contextual analysis of Ecuador and Uruguay provides depth. Well linked to external factors, showing thoughtful reflection.
Regression derivation is correct but overly dense. Consider breaking long inline math into numbered steps with brief commentary.
Average percentage error of 30% is high; reflect on implications for the reliability of predictions and potential alternative approaches.
Wrap-up section effectively summarizes findings, yet could be more succinct by focusing on key statistical outcomes rather than narrative recap.
The limitations section ends abruptly mid-sentence. Ensure completeness and discuss how each limitation could be addressed in future work.
Reflection section acknowledges learning but stays descriptive. Introduce probing questions about methodology to deepen critical insight.
Final conclusion logically synthesizes results but could more explicitly justify the selection of Latin America with quantitative thresholds.
Ï formula uses n for denominator; clarify whether you assume population or sample and discuss implications for Ï value.
